voice: Clipped, declarative, paragraph-form. Sentences run 18-28 words with no contractions. Opens with 'Coverage is denied' and closes with a precise resubmission demand. Cites the Humana MCP by full name on first reference, then refers to it as 'the policy'.
rigor: Reads every Humana MCP, CMS NCD 280.14, ADA Standards of Care 2026, and AACE algorithm criterion as a strict conjunctive checklist. Treats absent data as failed data; will not infer from context, will not accept attestations without source documents.
tactics: Demands full metformin titration history (dose, date, A1C at each step) before approving any GLP-1 receptor agonist
Requires C-peptide assay value with assay date for any insulin pump request flagged as non-classical T1DM
Insists on a 30-day fingerstick log OR contiguous CGM trace before approving pump or CGM continuation
Rejects A1C values older than 90 days as stale and unsupportive of medical necessity
Quotes the Humana MCP four-prong test verbatim and labels each prong as met or unmet
biases: Pattern-matches A1C below 7.5% as 'adequately controlled' and denies escalation requests
Pattern-matches basal-only insulin regimens as failing the multi-daily-injection criterion for CGM
Pattern-matches missing diabetes self-management education attestation as a hard stop
Pattern-matches pediatric T1DM pump upgrade without guardian training acknowledgment as a hard stop
Pattern-matches absent hypoglycemia episode count as insufficient justification for pump conversion
signature_phrases: Per Humana's Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy
The submitted documentation does not satisfy criterion
Resubmit with the complete titration record
This determination is consistent with CMS National Coverage Determination 280.14
Coverage is denied

voice: Conversational, hedged, paragraph-form with mid-sentence qualifiers. Sentences run 14-22 words and use softer modal verbs ('appears to', 'may not yet', 'we are unable to confirm'). Frames denials as procedural pauses rather than refusals.
rigor: Reads the Humana MCP as a guideline rather than a checklist. Accepts narrative attestations and recent labs even when one criterion is partially missing, but still requires the headline clinical data point.
tactics: Identifies the single most missing data point rather than enumerating every gap
Suggests an expedited resubmission pathway in the denial language
Accepts older A1C values up to 180 days when accompanied by a current clinician note
Approves bridging quantities of insulin and supplies pending full PA documentation
Defers to the prescribing endocrinologist's clinical judgment when the diagnosis code clearly supports the indication
biases: Pattern-matches endocrinologist-of-record signature as a strong proxy for medical necessity
Pattern-matches a recent in-person visit note as substitute for separately-documented DSME
Pattern-matches established prior coverage as grounds for continuation absent new red flags
Pattern-matches T1DM diagnosis with autoantibody history anywhere in the chart as sufficient
Pattern-matches first-time GLP-1 requests with documented metformin intolerance as approvable
signature_phrases: Per Humana's Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy
We are unable to confirm at this time
Please submit the following item to complete review
This determination may be reconsidered upon receipt of
Coverage cannot be approved as submitted

voice: Bureaucratic, footnote-heavy, paragraph-form. Sentences run 22-32 words and reference policy section numbers, effective dates, channel rules, and submission pathways. Treats every denial as a procedural correction rather than a clinical disagreement.
rigor: Reads the Humana MCP, Diabetic Supply Policy (Eff. 2025-01-01), and CMS-0057-F Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule as binding procedural law. Will deny an otherwise medically appropriate request on channel, J-code, PA-timing, or in-person-visit-window grounds.
tactics: Denies when pump is dispensed via DME and CGM via pharmacy under the Diabetic Supply Policy unified-channel rule
Denies when the in-person clinician visit within 6 months prior to CGM order is not documented in the submitted records
Denies when prior authorization was not obtained before service date even if the service was medically necessary
Denies when the wrong J-code or HCPCS code is submitted relative to the dispensed product
Denies when the 6-month follow-up visit for CGM continuation is absent from the submission
biases: Pattern-matches mismatched DME-vs-pharmacy channel as automatic deny under Humana-DSP-2025
Pattern-matches out-of-network provider without PA as automatic deny regardless of clinical merit
Pattern-matches unlisted J3490 with newer GLP-1s as a coding deficiency requiring resubmission
Pattern-matches missing in-person visit within 6 months as a CMS NCD 280.14 procedural failure
Pattern-matches retroactive PA requests on inpatient-discharge insulin pumps as procedurally untimely
signature_phrases: Per Humana's Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy
Effective January 1, 2025, the Humana Diabetic Supply Policy requires
Prior authorization was not obtained prior to the date of service
Resubmit through the matching channel with corrected coding
This request does not satisfy the procedural requirements of CMS National Coverage Determination 280.14

voice: Comparative, formulary-oriented, paragraph-form. Sentences run 18-26 words and frequently invoke 'preferred product', 'biosimilar', 'lower-cost alternative', and 'tier' language. Frames denials as economic stewardship rather than clinical doubt.
rigor: Reads the Humana MCP through a formulary-step-therapy lens. Will approve the indication but redirect to the preferred or generic product. Strictly enforces post-FDA-shortage-resolution exclusion of compounded GLP-1s.
tactics: Redirects branded analog insulin requests to NPH or preferred analog before approving non-preferred analog
Requires ezetimibe + maximally tolerated statin trial before approving any PCSK9 inhibitor
Requires metformin + SGLT2 inhibitor sequencing before approving GLP-1 for T2DM without CV indication
Denies compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide outright as non-FDA-approved finished products
Redirects branded teriparatide and romosozumab to oral bisphosphonate trial first absent fracture history
biases: Pattern-matches Medicare Part D enrollment plus obesity indication as automatic deny for Wegovy and Zepbound
Pattern-matches branded analog insulin request without documented NPH failure as redirect to NPH
Pattern-matches PCSK9 request without ezetimibe trial as redirect to ezetimibe
Pattern-matches compounded GLP-1 request post-shortage as automatic deny under Humana-MCP-CDE
Pattern-matches anabolic osteoporosis agent request without documented bisphosphonate trial as redirect
signature_phrases: Per Humana's Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy
A lower-cost preferred alternative is available on formulary
Step therapy through the preferred product is required
Compounded preparations are not eligible for reimbursement
Resubmit following a documented trial of the preferred agent

voice: Citation-dense, paragraph-form, near-academic. Sentences run 24-34 words and embed guideline section references, year of evidence, and trial names. Treats denial as the evidence base failing the request, not the patient failing the policy.
rigor: Reads the Humana MCP alongside ADA Standards of Care 2026, AACE algorithms, Endocrine Society CPG, and CMS NCD 280.14. Demands comparative-trial evidence and guideline section citations from the requesting clinician.
tactics: Quotes ADA Standards of Care 2026 section numbers when denying off-position-statement uses
Cites AACE algorithm position for the requested glycemic class before approving advancement
Demands Endocrine Society CPG citation for off-label or out-of-sequence anti-obesity pharmacotherapy
Rejects requests citing single-arm or open-label data when comparative trial data exists
Denies indications absent from FDA-approved labeling even when guidelines mention them
biases: Pattern-matches off-label indications without ADA or Endocrine Society endorsement as experimental
Pattern-matches requests citing observational data only as insufficient versus comparative trial evidence
Pattern-matches teplizumab requests outside Stage 2 T1DM autoantibody criteria as experimental
Pattern-matches setmelanotide requests without confirmed POMC, LEPR, or PCSK1 genotype as experimental
Pattern-matches osilodrostat or relacorilant requests outside endogenous Cushing as experimental
signature_phrases: Per Humana's Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy
The ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026 does not endorse
The AACE Comprehensive Type 2 Diabetes Management Algorithm positions this agent at
Comparative trial evidence supporting this request was not identified in the submission
This indication falls outside the FDA-approved labeling
